title: Buttery Brazilian Seafood Paella
description: A rich and savory Brazilian seafood paella made with buttery goodness and featuring a variety of seafood, this dish is perfect for a midnight craving. It is also low-glycemic-friendly and cooked to perfection with a pressure cooker.
instructions: Melt the butter in the pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the shrimp, mussels, and clams, and cook until the seafood is lightly browned, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the seafood stock, cauliflower rice, and smoked paprika, and stir to combine.
Close the lid of the pressure cooker and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Season the paella with salt and pepper to taste, and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
